name: tidy-find-copies
title: Duplication Scan
description: Find behavior-level, structure-level, or logic-level duplication in a bounded scope. Use when a module, folder, or repo needs explicit duplication analysis before simplification or refactoring.
purpose: Identify duplicated logic or structure that can be consolidated without changing behavior.
when_to_use: Need evidence-backed duplication findings before simplification or refactoring.
Need to know whether similar code is accidental copy-paste or justified variation.
Need a bounded duplication report for one module, folder, or repo.
do_not_use: Need a general review without duplication focus.
Need to implement refactoring immediately without first identifying duplication boundaries.
The target scope is too broad to compare meaningfully without narrowing.

required_inputs.name: DUPLICATION_KIND
required_inputs.description: Which kind of duplication to prioritize.

structured_outputs.name: DUPLICATION_CLUSTERS
structured_outputs.description: Grouped duplication candidates with evidence locations.

structured_outputs.name: KEEP_SEPARATE_REASONS
structured_outputs.description: Reasons similar-looking code should remain separate.

neutrality_rules: Treat similar code as duplication only when behavior or structure truly overlaps.
Separate safe merge opportunities from look-alike but justified variation.
Do not recommend abstraction when it would hide useful domain differences.
default_program: [stages: preflight>detect>analyze>review>audit | scope: repo|diff|paths(glob,...) | policy: evidence,deterministic-output | lens: hickey-carmack | output: md(contract=v1)]
procedure: Identify repeated flows, logic blocks, or structurally parallel modules in the target scope.
Group them into duplication clusters based on behavior, not only syntax.
Separate clusters that are safe to consolidate from cases where domain differences matter.
mandatory_rules: Report evidence locations for every duplication cluster.
Do not call parallel structure a bug by default.
lens_rationale: Duplication scanning should distinguish real duplication from justified variation.
